pulls.title: Identity Continuation Assertion for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange (-00)
pulls.author: mcguinness
pulls.body:
Adds the initial Internet-Draft **draft-mcguinness-oauth-id-continuation-assertion-00**.

## Summary
Profiles OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange (RFC 8693) to define the **Identity Continuation Assertion**: a short-lived, sender-constrained JWT that carries verifiable delegation-chain evidence to a continuation IdP, so the IdP can resolve the next audience's pairwise subject and issue an onward ID-JAG. Primary use case is same-IdP SaaS-to-SaaS chaining where the user is no longer present after the first hop.

## Contents
- Token type `urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:identity-continuation` + media type `application/oauth-identity-continuation+jwt`
- `chain_id` as a control-plane, non-bearer correlation handle (kept out of every data-plane token), anchored to the `sid`/`txn`/`grant_id` pattern
- Token Exchange profile (direct vs chained), sender-constrained presentation (DPoP or mTLS), sender-constrained `actor_token`
- IdP validation rules, atomic single-use `jti` consumption, chain lifetime + revocation, chain-context provenance
- IANA registrations (OAuth URI, media type, JWT claim `chain_id`, token-response parameter)
- Appendices: Design Rationale (why not ID-JAG / Transaction Token / cross-domain propagation) and a worked two-hop example
- Removes the i-d-template placeholder; adds `.gitignore` for build artifacts

Builds cleanly via `kramdown-rfc | xml2rfc`.

## Open item
The **Waffles** reference (`draft-hingnikar-cecchetti-wimse-waffles`) is not yet on the Datatracker; its title/URL are a placeholder marked with a NOTE and must be replaced with the authoritative citation.
